reasoning:
The reference price on June 8 is the close on June 6 (93.34). Ameren's stock goes ex-dividend by $0.71 on June 10, meaning 93.34 is at the very peak of the dividend accrual 'sawtooth' cycle. On June 10, the stock will mechanically drop by $0.71 at the open. 
- For the first resolution (June 15): There are only 5 trading days after June 6. The $0.71 drop creates a significant headwind. Given a daily volatility around 1.5, overcoming this short-term hurdle is unlikely (estimated Z-score ~ -0.2 to -0.3), putting the probability around 38%.
- For the second (July 8): The stock has about 1 month to accrue new dividend value (~$0.24) and capital drift, meaning the net penalty falls to roughly -$0.45. With 1 month of variance, probability sits around 46%.
- For Sept 6 and Dec 5: Both dates fall just days before Ameren's expected Q3 and Q4 mid-month ex-dividend dates (historically around Sept 11 and Dec 11). Thus, they are also at peak dividend accrual, cancelling out the mechanical headwind seen in the June comparison. We fall back to expecting mild positive capital appreciation drift over 3-6 months. Due to growing time-horizon variance, probabilities drift slightly above 0.50.
